title: Bump common to v0.66.1
body: Bump common to v0.66.1 to get some Windows updates in for Podman v5.7.1

comments.body: Should we have a branch for this? 5.8 will be a long-term support release and should be based on the same code as 5.7
@mheon I think we should, and I thought @Luap99 or @jankaluza had already made one.  I think the name was settled as podman-v5.7, but I'm not 100% sure on that.
And FWIW, I think we can push this through now, create the tag, then the new branch after it's out.

A `podman-5.7` branch _already exists_ (with 2 brackports); if the goal for this is to land in 5.7.x, it should happen on that branch — or that branch should be moved and/or deleted. (I don’t know / care much what do we do, but let’s deal with the _ambiguity_ now before we make any public tags.)
This MUST be cut of the podman-5.7 branch we have changes in main that are only supposed to go into 6.0

OH DOH!

I was looking for a release, not a branch.  Too much staring at the screen lately.  Sorry folks, I'll rework.
